narrative_md: FHIR Device and DeviceUseStatement together map to one OMOP device_exposure row. Device carries identity (type, UDI, manufacturer, lot/serial); DeviceUseStatement records the clinical event (who, when, why). Alternatively, Procedure.usedCode can be a source. The mapper must resolve the two-resource join: DeviceUseStatement.device references a Device resource from which type, UDI, and production identifiers are extracted.

fields.notes: Surrogate key. Autogenerated sequence or hash of FHIR resource ID.
Resolve Patient reference to integer person_id. DeviceUseStatement.subject is 1..1 and restricted to Patient.
Resolve Device.type.coding to a Standard Concept in the Device domain via vocabulary lookup. SNOMED device codes preferred. If no standard concept found, use 0.

sources.comment: Device.type coding vocab lookup from contained Device; getCodingFirstRep() — DeviceUseStatement+Device pattern
Procedure.usedCode coding SNOMED lookup — no DeviceUseStatement, no Device resource needed
No concept mapping (field not populated — PySpark from Procedure)

fields.transform: If timingPeriod: use Period.start. If timingDateTime: use that value. If timingTiming: use bounds start or first event. Fallback to recordedOn if timing absent.
fields.notes: Required NOT NULL. If no date can be determined, the record cannot be created.

sources.comment: timingPeriod.start from DeviceUseStatement (Period is the primary pattern)
Procedure.performed[x] start (ETL-German uses Procedure as source, not DeviceUseStatement)

fields.notes: Full datetime precision of the start.
If timingPeriod: use Period.end. If timingDateTime: null (point-in-time). If timing absent: null.
Full datetime precision of the end.
Provenance concept. Use 32817 (EHR) for EHR-sourced data. omoponfhir uses 44818707 (EHR order list entry).

sources.comment: 44818707 (EHR order list entry) — hardcoded in omoponfhir

fields.notes: The UDI Device Identifier (UDI-DI). Take from the first udiCarrier entry. This is the fixed portion identifying the device model.

The UDI Production Identifier (UDI-PI). OMOP v5.4 field. Combine lot number and/or serial number. If both present, prefer lotNumber. Some implementations concatenate: lot:{lotNumber};serial:{serialNumber}.
Not directly available from DeviceUseStatement. May be derived from Procedure context or set to 1 as default.
Resolve Practitioner/PractitionerRole reference to integer provider_id. Only Practitioner maps cleanly to OMOP provider. Patient/RelatedPerson references are skipped.

DeviceUseStatement has no direct encounter reference. Resolve via derivedFrom (Procedure with encounter) or temporal overlap with visit_occurrence records. ETL-German gets this from parent Procedure.encounter.
Same resolution strategy as visit_occurrence_id but at the detail level. Rarely populated.
Verbatim source code. omoponfhir encodes as system:code when concept lookup fails. ETL-German uses the raw code value. Truncate if exceeds 50 chars.

sources.comment: system:code composite string when vocab lookup fails (may exceed varchar(50))
Raw code value only (no system prefix)

fields.notes: The source concept_id before standardization. If a non-standard concept matches the source coding, use it; otherwise 0. ETL-German sets this equal to device_concept_id.
For devices with measurable quantities (e.g., blood transfusion volume). Not typically available from Device/DeviceUseStatement.
Verbatim unit string if applicable. Not typically available.
Source concept for the unit. Default 0.

entries.notes: When Device.type is absent or code cannot be mapped
Default for EHR-sourced FHIR data. Used by ETL-German.
Direct lookup in CONCEPT table; filter to domain_id = 'Device'. Standard concepts map directly.
Requires source_to_concept_map or custom vocabulary load. Not in default OMOP vocabulary bundle.
UDI-DI is an identifier, not a coded concept. Store in unique_device_id, not device_concept_id.
May appear in Procedure context. Map via standard_concept relationships.

references.notes: Logical model only (draft). No FML transform map exists for DeviceExposure.
Bidirectional. constructOmop (F->O) handles contained Device resolution, timingPeriod, Device.type vocab lookup, udiCarrier, device_type_concept_id=44818707.
O->F read-only (270 lines). constructFHIR builds Device from device_exposure entity.
Maps Procedure.usedCode to device_exposure. No DeviceUseStatement mapper. Uses SNOMED concept lookup. device_type_concept_id=32817.
Earlier version, same logic as omoponfhir-v54-r4.
Legacy DSTU2. O->F read-only. Maps entity to FHIR Device (not DeviceUseStatement).
PySpark. Maps FHIR Procedure (not Device/DeviceUseStatement) to device_exposure. Minimal field coverage. Status: abandoned.

edge_cases.handling: device_concept_id = 0, device_source_value = null. Log warning. The record is still valid -- OMOP allows concept 0.
Use the dateTime for device_exposure_start_date. device_exposure_end_date = null (point-in-time exposure).
No natural OMOP representation. Options: (a) use Timing.repeat.boundsPeriod as start/end, (b) create one row per event occurrence, (c) skip and log warning. No implementation handles this case.
Fall back to DeviceUseStatement.recordedOn for start date. If that is also absent, the record cannot be created (device_exposure_start_date is NOT NULL). Skip and log.
Take the first entry's deviceIdentifier for unique_device_id. OMOP has a single field; additional UDI carriers are lost.
Do not create a device_exposure row. ETL-German filters on acceptable status values (completed, active, on-hold).
Must resolve the Device reference before mapping. If the Device resource is unavailable, log warning and set device_concept_id = 0, unique_device_id = null.
Create one device_exposure row per coding entry (ETL-German approach). Deduplicate if needed.
OMOP field is varchar(50). Truncate the source value. system:code format from omoponfhir may exceed this limit for long system URIs.

edge_cases.case: Device.type has multiple codings (e.g., SNOMED + GMDN)
edge_cases.handling: Use the first coding that maps to a standard OMOP concept. Store all codings in device_source_value or pick the primary one. omoponfhir uses getCodingFirstRep().

edge_cases.case: Implantable device with no end date
edge_cases.handling: device_exposure_end_date = null. This is correct for permanent implants -- the exposure is ongoing.
OMOP v5.4 added this field. Combine Device.lotNumber and/or Device.serialNumber. No standard format. Suggestion: lot:{value} or serial:{value} or concatenated.
